perturbation.original_text: 2.2.1 Applicable Services . Throughout the Term, ABCO shall have the right of first offer to provide the following services to Evolent and Evolent’s customers: (a) Physician practice management consulting and other physician practice management services; (b) Physician incentive design services; (c) Medical home development consulting services; and (d) Performance analytics shared with physicians with the intent to improve outcomes.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2.1 Applicable Services . Throughout the Term, ABCO shall not have the right of first offer to provide any services to Evolent and Evolent’s customers: (a) Physician practice management consulting and other physician practice management services; (b) Physician incentive design services; (c) Medical home development consulting services; and (d) Performance analytics shared with physicians with the intent to improve outcomes.
perturbation.explanation: Changing “shall have the right of first offer” to “shall not have the right of first offer” creates a contradiction in Section 2.4 that states 'Evolent is not precluded from offering and providing services directly to its customers, subject (a) to (i.e., after ABCO has had the full opportunity to exercise) ABCO’s right of first offer to provide any First Offer Services in accordance with Section 2.2)'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.4 Reservation of Rights : For the avoidance of doubt, Evolent is not precluded from offering and providing services directly to its customers, subject (a) to (i.e., after ABCO has had the full opportunity to exercise) ABCO’s right of first offer to provide any First Offer Services in accordance with Section 2.2) and (b) to Evolent’s compliance with its non-competition obligations as set forth in Section 6.2.
perturbation.justification: The original text grants ABCO the right of first offer for certain services. The changed text explicitly states that ABCO does NOT have the right of first offer for those same services. This directly contradicts Section 2.4, which states Evolent's right to provide services is 'subject to ABCO's right of first offer'. Therefore, by negating ABCO's right of first offer, the changed text creates a clear contradiction with Section 2.4, where Evolent's rights are conditional upon ABCO's right of first offer.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2 Minimum Purchase and Shortfall : Evolent agrees to pay ABCO the Minimum Purchase. If the aggregate amount of Services actually procured by Evolent from ABCO is not equal to or greater than (a) Seven Hundred Thousand Dollars ($700,000) of services for delivery on or prior to the second anniversary of the Original Effective Date or (b) inclusive of the purchase described in clause (a), the Minimum Purchase of services for delivery on or prior to the third anniversary of the Original Effective Date, Evolent shall pay ABCO the amount of the shortfall (i.e., the difference between Seven Hundred Thousand Dollars ($700,000) or the Minimum Purchase amount, as applicable, less the sum of: (a) the aggregate fees for the actual amount of Services procured; and (b) any Credits (as provided in the next sentence) (the “ Shortfall ”), if any, on or before August 31, 2013 or August 31, 2014, as applicable. In the event that ABCO refuses or fails, without reasonable cause, to provide any of the Services requested by Evolent (provided the scope of the Services requested is within the scope customarily provided by ABCO), the fees that would otherwise have been applicable had ABCO agreed to provide such Services would count as a “ Credit ”. As of the date hereof, no such Credits have accrued.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2 Minimum Purchase and Shortfall : Evolent agrees to pay ABCO the Minimum Purchase. If the aggregate amount of Services actually procured by Evolent from ABCO is not equal to or greater than (a) Seven Hundred Thousand Dollars ($700,000) of services for delivery on or prior to the second anniversary of the Original Effective Date or (b) inclusive of the purchase described in clause (a), the Minimum Purchase of services for delivery on or prior to the third anniversary of the Original Effective Date, Evolent shall not pay ABCO the amount of the shortfall (i.e., the difference between Seven Hundred Thousand Dollars ($700,000) or the Minimum Purchase amount, as applicable, less the sum of: (a) the aggregate fees for the actual amount of Services procured; and (b) any Credits (as provided in the next sentence) (the “ Shortfall ”), if any, on or before August 31, 2013 or August 31, 2014, as applicable. In the event that ABCO refuses or fails, without reasonable cause, to provide any of the Services requested by Evolent (provided the scope of the Services requested is within the scope customarily provided by ABCO), the fees that would otherwise have been applicable had ABCO agreed to provide such Services would not count as a “ Credit ”. As of the date hereof, no such Credits have accrued.
perturbation.explanation: By changing “Evolent shall pay ABCO the amount of the shortfall” to “Evolent shall not pay ABCO the amount of the shortfall”, this creates an in-text contradiction by negating Evolent's obligation to pay any shortfall, directly conflicting with the initial statement, stating that Evolent agrees to pay ABCO the Minimum Purchase.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Minimum Purchase and Shortfall : Evolent agrees to pay ABCO the Minimum Purchase. If the aggregate amount of Services actually procured by Evolent from ABCO is not equal to or greater than (a) Seven Hundred Thousand Dollars ($700,000) of services for delivery on or prior to the second anniversary of the Original Effective Date or (b) inclusive of the purchase described in clause (a), the Minimum Purchase of services for delivery on or prior to the third anniversary of the Original Effective Date, Evolent shall not pay ABCO the amount of the shortfall (i.e., the difference between Seven Hundred Thousand Dollars ($700,000) or the Minimum Purchase amount, as applicable, less the sum of: (a) the aggregate fees for the actual amount of Services procured; and (b) any Credits (as provided in the next sentence) (the “ Shortfall ”), if any, on or before August 31, 2013 or August 31, 2014, as applicable. In the event that ABCO refuses or fails, without reasonable cause, to provide any of the Services requested by Evolent (provided the scope of the Services requested is within the scope customarily provided by ABCO), the fees that would otherwise have been applicable had ABCO agreed to provide such Services would not count as a “ Credit ”. As of the date hereof, no such Credits have accrued.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the initial statement of Section 3.2. The original text states "Evolent shall pay ABCO the amount of the shortfall", meaning that Evolent is obligated to pay the shortfall to ABCO. The changed text states "Evolent shall not pay ABCO the amount of the shortfall", meaning Evolent is no longer obligated to pay the amount of the shortfall to ABCO. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict, as well as conflicting implications within the same section, aligning with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4. TERM AND RENEWAL . The initial term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and unless sooner terminated in accordance with the terms hereof, shall continue until August 31, 2016 (“ Initial Term ”). Thereafter, this Agreement shall automatically be renewed for another five-year term (“ Renewal Term ”) unless either Party elects to terminate this Agreement by providing the other Party with written notice of termination one hundred eighty (180) days prior to the expiration of the current term. (“ Initial Term ” and “ Renewal Term ” collectively, the “ Term ”).
perturbation.changed_text: 4. TERM AND RENEWAL . The initial term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and unless sooner terminated in accordance with the terms hereof, shall continue until August 31, 2016 (“ Initial Term ”). Thereafter, this Agreement shall automatically terminate and not be renewed. (“ Initial Term ” and “ Renewal Term ” collectively, the “ Term ”).
perturbation.explanation: By removing the renewal term and stating that the agreement will automatically terminate, we create an in-text contradiction to what was previously stated in the same section, where the agreement automatically renews. This change also contradicts sections that reference the term length like 6.1.1, where ABCO agrees not to offer any ABCO Restricted Products and Services throughout the term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. TERM AND RENEWAL . The initial term of this Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and unless sooner terminated in accordance with the terms hereof, shall continue until August 31, 2016 (“ Initial Term ”). Thereafter, this Agreement shall automatically terminate and not be renewed. (“ Initial Term ” and “ Renewal Term ” collectively, the “ Term ”).
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the automatic renewal provision and explicitly states the agreement will terminate, contradicting the original text which allowed for automatic renewal. As explained by the prompt, this also creates contradiction to sections such as 6.1.1, where ABCO agrees not to offer any ABCO Restricted Products and Services throughout the term. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text but the changed text now directly conflicts with the possibility of the agreement being renewed.

perturbation.original_text: 6.3 Non-Solicitation or Hiring : During the Term and for a period of eighteen (18) months thereafter, Evolent shall not solicit or hire any of ABCO’s employees without ABCO’s prior written consent.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.3 Non-Solicitation or Hiring : Evolent shall solicit and hire any of ABCO’s employees without ABCO’s prior written consent, during and after the Term .
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Evolent shall not solicit or hire any of ABCO’s employees' to 'Evolent shall solicit and hire any of ABCO’s employees' directly contradicts section 14.2 that states: 'Each Party shall have the sole and exclusive control over the labor and employee relations policies and policies relating to wages, hours, working conditions, benefits, or other conditions of its personnel and shall be responsible and liable for the acts and omissions of its employees, agents and contractors.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14.2 Independent Contractor : The relationship of ABCO to Evolent shall at all times be that of an independent contractor. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed to create any partnership, association, joint venture, or employment between the Parties. Each Party shall have the sole and exclusive control over the labor and employee relations policies and policies relating to wages, hours, working conditions, benefits, or other conditions of its personnel and shall be responsible and liable for the acts and omissions of its employees, agents and contractors.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that Evolent shall not solicit or hire ABCO's employees. The changed text states that Evolent shall solicit and hire ABCO's employees, which is a direct contradiction. This contradicts section 14.2, which gives each party sole control over their employee relations, effectively preventing one party from forcing the other to solicit/hire its employees. Therefore, the change creates a direct and strong contradiction.
